fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Dakota Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to south dakota, speech-language pathologist compact south dakota, south dakota speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Hearing Aid Dispensers and Audiologists
board.mailing_address:
South Dakota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
810 N. Main St., Suite 203
Spearfish, SD 57783

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in South Dakota.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Register or Log In to MyLicense Portal
steps.description: Go to the South Dakota MyLicense Portal (mylc.sd.gov). New applicants must register for an account, while returning users can log in with their existing credentials.

steps.title: Start a New Application
steps.description: Once logged in to the MyLicense Portal, locate the 'South Dakota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology' and select 'Start a New Application' for the Speech-Language Pathologist license type.

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application within the MyLicense Portal, including personal information, education history, supervised clinical experience details, and professional background.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents directly through the MyLicense Portal. This typically includes official transcripts from your graduate program, documentation of supervised clinical experience (e.g., ASHA Clinical Fellowship Report), and any other forms specified by the Board.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee using a credit or debit card through the secure payment system integrated within the MyLicense Portal.

steps.title: Ensure Praxis Exam Scores are Sent
steps.description: Arrange for your official Praxis exam scores (Speech-Language Pathology) to be sent directly to the South Dakota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology. The ETS institution code for South Dakota is 7904.

telepractice_text: South Dakota permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. South Dakota is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: South Dakota issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: South Dakota does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a South Dakota SLP license cost?
Is South Dakota part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does South Dakota allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a valid SD license.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. South Dakota participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in South Dakota without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: South Dakota does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in South Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for an SLP license in South Dakota, you must submit your application through the South Dakota MyLicense Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the South Dakota MyLicense Portal URL. New users must click 'Register' to create an account by providing personal details and creating a password. Existing users should 'Login' with their credentials. Once logged in, select 'Start a New Application' under the South Dakota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology, and choose the Speech-Language Pathologist license type.
